body.title: test_renders_status_groups asserts raw status strings vs canonical display labels
body.description:
Pre-existing failure (confirmed independent of the akien-bucket fix): tests/test_web_queue.py::TestPageQueue::test_renders_status_groups asserts the raw status strings 'in_progress'/'sprint'/'hold' appear in the rendered /queue HTML, but the status-model display work (queue_view._STATUS_LABEL, e.g. in_progress->'In progress') now renders canonical display labels, so the raw 'in_progress' literal is no longer guaranteed in the markup. Fix the TEST to match the canonical 7-status display vocab (or assert on the label/data attribute), not the code.

**Affected files:** tests/test_web_queue.py (test_renders_status_groups); reference lab/claudecode/queue_view.py (_STATUS_LABEL) + devices/web_server/server.py render path.
**Design rules:** test-plan; no-sqlite n/a.
**Scope boundary:** IN - update the assertion to the canonical display labels (or stable data attrs) and confirm the fixture _TICKET_ROWS exercises in_progress/sprint/hold. OUT - changing the renderer or the status model.
**Test plan:** test_renders_status_groups passes; full tests/test_web_queue.py green.

## Escalation summary
**What was tried:** {"status": "error", "result": "MAX_TURNS: hit 80 turns without terminal response", "error_class": "MAX_TURNS", "error_number": 80}
**Where it broke:** result missing DONE: prefix — not a completion
**What now?**
body.result: CC-validated: DS fix green under canonical labels
